DPR: Compile data on "Weird Al" Yankovic's film roles (1970s–2000s) alongside corresponding film financials (1954–1974) to analyze how his roles intersect with industry revenue trends. Compare revenue and admissions metrics of films from 1954 (international markets), 1957 (country of origin), and 1974 (domestic gross) to assess longitudinal changes in box office success. Cross-analyze 1955–1964 musical compositions (performers, locations) with 1970 country music chart positions to identify correlations between regional music success and film production trends. Evaluate whether specific film roles, studios, or directors are statistically associated with higher revenue or admissions across eras, and contrast these patterns with regional music chart performances. This dataset should enable queries on cross-decade cultural-economic linkages and role-based financial predictors.
sub_questions: How do musical compositions from 1955 to 1964 correlate with country music chart positions in 1970?
What were the revenue and admissions for films in 1954, 1957, and 1974?
What film roles did 'Weird Al' Yankovic have in the 1970s and 2000s?

subquery_results.sub_question: How do musical compositions from 1955 to 1964 correlate with country music chart positions in 1970?

attempts.sql: SELECT Composer, Composition, Date, Location, Performers FROM T9 WHERE Date BETWEEN '1955-01-01' AND '1964-12-31' UNION ALL SELECT Composer, Composition, Date, Location, Performers FROM T11 WHERE Date BETWEEN '1955-01-01' AND '1964-12-31'

DPR:
Compile a dataset tracking changes in the entertainment industry over time, including:  
1. Analyze revenue and admissions trends in films from 1954–1974 to identify financial growth or decline patterns.  
2. Examine "Weird Al" Yankovic’s career progression from the 1970s to 2000s, mapping how roles in films/TV evolved.  
3. Assess shifts in popular music through country chart rankings (1970) and film composers’ works (1955–1964), identifying how styles or market dominance changed.  
4. Correlate box office performance of 1974 films with studio strategies to evaluate industry competition dynamics.  
This should provide insights into entertainment sector evolution across decades, cultural shifts, and career trajectories.
sub_questions: How do roles in T1 and T2 change over time for a given title or role?
What revenue and admissions trends appear in T10 for films between 1954 and 1974?
What range of Domestic_gross values appears in T13 for films from 1974?

subquery_results.sub_question: How do roles in T1 and T2 change over time for a given title or role?

attempts.sql: SELECT Year, Title, Role FROM T1 WHERE Title LIKE '%Tapeheads%' OR Role LIKE '%Himself%' UNION ALL SELECT Year, Title, Role FROM T2 WHERE Title LIKE '%Laverne & Shirley%' OR Role LIKE '%Keyboard Player%' ORDER BY Year LIMIT 100

subquery_results.final_sql: SELECT Year, Title, Role FROM T1 WHERE Title LIKE '%Tapeheads%' OR Role LIKE '%Himself%' UNION ALL SELECT Year, Title, Role FROM T2 WHERE Title LIKE '%Laverne & Shirley%' OR Role LIKE '%Keyboard Player%' ORDER BY Year LIMIT 100
subquery_results.mini_summary: Roles in "Tapeheads" and "The Naked Gun : From the Files of Police Squad !" both involve "Himself" in the year 1988.

subquery_results.sub_question: What revenue and admissions trends appear in T10 for films between 1954 and 1974?

attempts.sql: SELECT Title, Studio, CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(Revenue, '$',''), ',', ''), ' ', '') AS REAL) AS Revenue, CAST(REPLACE(Admissions, ',', '') AS REAL) AS Admissions FROM T10 WHERE Title LIKE '%The%' AND Country LIKE '%France%' AND CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(Revenue, '$',''), ',', ''), ' ', '') AS REAL) > 0 AND CAST(REPLACE(Admissions, ',', '') AS REAL) > 0

SELECT 
    Title, 
    Studio, 
    CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(Revenue, '$',''), ',', ''), ' ', '') AS REAL) AS Revenue, 
    CAST(REPLACE(Admissions, ',', '') AS REAL) AS Admissions 
FROM 
    T10 
WHERE 
    CAST(SUBSTR(Title, LENGTH(Title) - 3, LENGTH(Title)) AS REAL) BETWEEN 1954 AND 1974

SELECT 
    Title, 
    Studio, 
    CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(Revenue, '$',''), ',', ''), ' ', '') AS REAL) AS Revenue, 
    CAST(REPLACE(Admissions, ',', '') AS REAL) AS Admissions 
FROM 
    T10 
WHERE 
    Title LIKE '%19%' AND CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(Revenue, '$',''), ',', ''), ' ', '') AS REAL) > 0 AND CAST(REPLACE(Admissions, ',', '') AS REAL) > 0

DPR: Compile a dataset that analyzes the financial and cultural performance of film/music projects across decades, comparing revenue/admissions data with artist involvement (e.g., "Weird Al" Yankovic's roles in films) to inform strategic decisions for production studios. The data should support: 1) Identifying market preferences by comparing film/music revenue trends and artist career impacts; 2) Evaluating international market performance to prioritize studio investments; 3) Assessing historical chart success patterns (e.g., 1970 country singles) to guide marketing strategies. The dataset must include metadata on roles, production years, geographic markets, revenue figures, and studio performance metrics to enable actionable cross-industry insights.
sub_questions: How do roles in T1 correlate with revenue in T10 for specific titles?
Which studios have the highest admissions in T8 across different international markets?
What revenue trends appear in T10 for films with specific directors?

subquery_results.sub_question: How do roles in T1 correlate with revenue in T10 for specific titles?

attempts.sql:
SELECT T1.Title, T1.Role, CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(T10.Revenue, '$',''), ',', ''), ' ', '') AS REAL) AS Revenue 
FROM T1 
JOIN T10 
ON T1.Title = T10.Title 
WHERE T1.Title LIKE '%UHF%' 
AND T10.Revenue NOT LIKE 'N/A' 
LIMIT 10

SELECT T1.Title, T1.Role, CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(T10.Revenue, '$',''), ',', ''), ' ', '') AS REAL) AS Revenue 
FROM T1 
JOIN T10 
ON T1.Title = T10.Title 
WHERE T10.Revenue NOT LIKE 'N/A' 
LIMIT 10

SELECT T2.Title, T2.Role, CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(T10.Revenue, '$',''), ',', ''), ' ', '') AS REAL) AS Revenue 
FROM T2 
JOIN T10 
ON T2.Title = T10.Title 
WHERE T10.Revenue NOT LIKE 'N/A' 
AND T2.Title LIKE '%UHF%' 
LIMIT 10

subquery_results.sub_question: Which studios have the highest admissions in T8 across different international markets?

attempts.sql: SELECT Studio, MAX(CAST(REPLACE(Admissions, ',', '') AS REAL)) AS max_admissions FROM T8 GROUP BY Studio ORDER BY max_admissions DESC LIMIT 1

subquery_results.final_sql: SELECT Studio, MAX(CAST(REPLACE(Admissions, ',', '') AS REAL)) AS max_admissions FROM T8 GROUP BY Studio ORDER BY max_admissions DESC LIMIT 1
subquery_results.mini_summary:
The studio with the highest admissions in T8 across different international markets is R. K. Films. 
R. K. Films has 100,000,000 admissions.

final_summary: A dataset analyzing film/music projects' financial and cultural performance could identify market preferences by comparing revenue trends and artist career impacts. However, the evidence does not support evaluating international market performance to prioritize studio investments, as it only mentions one studio with high admissions in a specific market. The dataset could also assess historical chart success patterns, but limitations are visible in the evidence as it does not provide information on chart success patterns.

DPR: Compile a standardized dataset of legislative members across British Columbia, Manitoba, and Puerto Rico from 1899 to 1972, mapping districts to members and their political affiliations. Structure the data to enable comparative analysis of party representation trends over time, regional differences in electoral district distribution, and correlations between jurisdictional political dynamics and legislative composition. Include contextual attributes like legislative term periods to analyze shifts in party alignment between geographic regions and historical periods.
sub_questions: What members are associated with each electoral district in T4?
What affiliations are associated with members in T31?
What parties are represented in each district in T58?

subquery_results.sub_question: What members are associated with each electoral district in T4?

attempts.sql: SELECT Member, Electoral_district FROM T4

subquery_results.mini_summary:
The result set does not show which electoral districts are associated with T4. 

The members associated with each electoral district in the result set are Harlan Carey Brewster (Alberni), Frank Harry Mobley (Atlin), John MacKay Yorston (Cariboo), Edward Dodsley Barrow (Chilliwack), and John Andrew Buckham (Columbia).

subquery_results.mini_summary:

mini_summaries:
The result set does not show which electoral districts are associated with T4. 

The members associated with each electoral district in the result set are Harlan Carey Brewster (Alberni), Frank Harry Mobley (Atlin), John MacKay Yorston (Cariboo), Edward Dodsley Barrow (Chilliwack), and John Andrew Buckham (Columbia).

The affiliations associated with members in T31 are Conservative. 

All 5 rows show the affiliation as Conservative.

The parties represented in District Num . V ( District 21-25 ) Ponce are PDP. 
The parties represented in Senator At-Large are NPP and PDP.
final_summary: A standardized dataset of legislative members can be compiled, but limitations are visible in the evidence as it does not show which electoral districts are associated with T4. The dataset can include affiliations, such as Conservative, and parties represented in specific districts, like PDP in District 21-25 and NPP and PDP in Senator At-Large. However, the affiliations of members in T31 are known to be Conservative, and the parties represented in other districts, like Alberni, Atlin, Cariboo, Chilliwack, and Columbia, are not specified.

UNION ALL
